overview.description: Glen Rock is a small, affluent borough in central Bergen County known for its top-rated public schools, tree-lined streets, and strong community spirit. Named after a large glacial boulder that once served as a landmark for the Lenape people, Glen Rock epitomizes the classic Bergen County commuter suburb. The borough offers NJ Transit rail service, excellent schools, a charming downtown, and a well-maintained network of parks and recreation facilities.
highlights: Top-rated public school district consistently ranked among New Jersey's best
NJ Transit train station on the Bergen County Line for NYC commuting
Named for a 570-ton glacial boulder (now in a small park on Rock Road)
Beautiful tree-lined streets with well-maintained homes
Active community with strong volunteer organizations and family events

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Glen Rock's housing stock consists primarily of single-family homes ranging from pre-war colonials to modern new construction.
realEstate.recentTrends: Glen Rock's real estate market is highly competitive, driven by the school district's reputation. Homes near the train station and downtown command premiums. Teardowns of older homes for new construction have increased, with new homes selling for over $1 million. Bidding wars are common for well-priced listings.
neighborhoodNotes: The area near Rock Road and the train station is the most walkable, with quick access to downtown shops and commuter rail
South Glen Rock features larger lots and homes near the Ridgewood border
The Harding Plaza area has the main shopping district with restaurants and local businesses
The northern section near Fair Lawn has some of the more affordable housing in the borough

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by the Borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by neighborhood — check the borough website or Recycle Coach app
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use sealed bags or covered containers not exceeding 50 lbs
Bulk item pickup available by scheduling with the DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream recycling is collected biweekly through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables (plastic, glass, cans) collected in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected on alternate weeks
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-soiled items
Yard waste collected seasonally in biodegradable bags; check DPW schedule

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water (formerly United Water) and sewer service is managed through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills issued quarterly by Veolia
Sewer charges appear on property tax or utility bills
Water quality reports published annually
Report water emergencies to Veolia or DPW

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Glen Rock Building Department handles construction, renovation, and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for structural, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC work
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board or Zoning Board of Adjustment
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and renovations
Tree removal permits required for trees over a certain size

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Glen Rock manages commuter parking at the train station and street parking in the downtown area.
details: Commuter parking permits available for the NJ Transit station lot
Metered parking available in the downtown commercial area
No overnight parking on borough streets (2:00 AM to 6:00 AM)
Free parking in municipal lots on weekends

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Recreation Department offers extensive youth and adult programs year-round.
details: Youth sports leagues including baseball, soccer, basketball, lacrosse, and swimming
Summer camp and recreation programs
Adult fitness classes and sports leagues
Community pool at the Glen Rock Pool facility (seasonal)

additionalServices.title: Library Services
additionalServices.description: The Glen Rock Public Library is a hub for community activities and resources.
details: Located at 315 Rock Road
Extensive children's programs, teen activities, and adult book clubs
Digital resources including e-books and streaming services
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Glen Rock enforces noise restrictions to maintain the quiet residential character of the borough.
keyRules: Construction work prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Power equipment (leaf blowers, mowers) restricted to 8:00 AM to 7:00 PM
Excessive noise complaints handled by the police department

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties to the borough's high residential standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be kept trimmed and landscaping maintained
Snow and ice must be cleared from sidewalks within 24 hours of storm ending
No accumulation of trash, debris, or abandoned vehicles
Fences must comply with height and setback regulations
Trees and shrubs must not obstruct sidewalks or sight lines

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed annually and controlled at all times.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed with the Borough Clerk by January 31 each year
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Owners must clean up after their pets on public and private property
Rabies vaccination required for all dogs and cats
Dog park available at Wildwood Park for off-leash play

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Glen Rock enforces parking regulations to maintain order on residential and commercial streets.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets (2:00 AM to 6:00 AM)
Commuter parking at the train station requires a borough-issued permit
Snow emergency parking bans enforced when declared
Fire hydrant clearance of 10 feet required
No parking on lawns or unpaved areas

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Glen Rock has limited rental housing; regulations maintain residential character.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before tenant occupancy
Fire safety requirements apply to all rental units
Security deposit limited to 1.5 months' rent per NJ state law
Tenants protected under NJ Anti-Eviction Act
rentals.source: Glen Rock Borough Code, NJ Landlord-Tenant Law

other.title: Tree Preservation
other.summary: Glen Rock has a strong tree preservation ordinance to maintain its tree-lined character.
keyRules: Permit required to remove any tree over 8 inches in diameter at breast height
Replacement planting required when trees are removed
The Shade Tree Commission reviews tree removal applications
Fines for unauthorized tree removal; street trees are borough property
other.source: Glen Rock Borough Code, Shade Tree Commission

publicSchools.name: Glen Rock High School
publicSchools.notes: Highly ranked high school with strong AP program, award-winning music and theater, and competitive athletics. Consistently ranked among top public high schools in NJ.

publicSchools.name: Central Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Excellent middle school with honors programs and a wide range of extracurricular activities

publicSchools.name: Byrd School
publicSchools.notes: Named after Admiral Richard E. Byrd; one of three elementary schools

privateSchools.name: Academy of Our Lady of Mount Carmel
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school affiliated with Our Lady of Mount Carmel Parish

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey — Mahwah (15 miles)

whyMoveHere: Top-rated public school district — one of the best in Bergen County and New Jersey
NJ Transit train station (Bergen County Line) for direct Hoboken/NYC commutes
Beautiful tree-lined streets with well-maintained homes and strong community pride
Charming downtown with local shops, restaurants, and community gathering spots
Outstanding parks, recreation programs, and community events for families

commuteToNYC.duration: 45-60 minutes by train to Hoboken + PATH; 35-55 minutes by bus
commuteToNYC.trainStation: Glen Rock — Main Line, Glen Rock — Boro Hall
commuteToNYC.notes: Glen Rock has two NJ Transit train stations (Main Line and Boro Hall) on the Bergen County Line. Trains run to Hoboken with connections to the PATH train for Manhattan. NJ Transit bus service also available. Car commute via Route 208 or Route 4 to the George Washington Bridge.

costOfLiving.summary: Glen Rock has a high cost of living driven by premium home prices and property taxes, reflecting the value placed on the excellent school system.

pros: Exceptional public schools — top-rated at all levels
Two NJ Transit train stations for convenient commuting
Beautiful, walkable neighborhoods with mature trees
Strong community spirit with active civic organizations
cons: High property taxes — over 3% effective rate
Premium home prices driven by school district demand
Train commute to Manhattan requires a transfer at Hoboken
Limited dining and nightlife options in the small downtown
Competition for homes can lead to bidding wars

restaurants.name: Holsten's
restaurants.description: Iconic ice cream parlor and restaurant famous as the filming location of the final scene of The Sopranos. Classic American diner food and legendary ice cream sundaes.

restaurants.name: Kinchley's Tavern
restaurants.description: Legendary thin-crust pizza institution serving Glen Rock since 1937. Known for its signature thin, crispy bar-style pizza.

restaurants.name: Glen Rock Inn
restaurants.description: Classic American restaurant offering steak, seafood, and pasta in a traditional setting that has served the community for decades.

restaurants.name: La Chinita Poblana
restaurants.description: Authentic Mexican restaurant serving traditional Puebla-style dishes, homemade mole, and fresh tortillas.

parks.name: Thielke Arboretum
parks.description: A peaceful 11-acre nature preserve with walking trails, native plants, and educational gardens maintained by the Glen Rock Environmental Commission.

parks.name: Wilde Memorial Park
parks.description: Glen Rock's main park with ball fields, basketball courts, playgrounds, a gazebo, and space for community events.

parks.name: The Rock (Glen Rock)
parks.description: A 570-ton glacial erratic boulder that gives the borough its name. The rock sits in a small park on Rock Road and is a local landmark.

parks.name: Saddle River County Park — Glen Rock Section
parks.description: A section of the linear Bergen County park along the Saddle River, with walking and biking paths and nature areas.
parks.address: Along Saddle River, Glen Rock, NJ

events.name: Glen Rock Street Fair
events.description: Annual street fair along Rock Road featuring vendors, food, live music, and activities for the whole family.

events.name: Glen Rock Day
events.description: Community celebration with games, rides, food, and entertainment at Wilde Memorial Park.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual tree lighting at the Borough Hall with caroling, hot chocolate, and a festive community gathering.

events.name: Summer Concert Series
events.description: Free outdoor concerts in Wilde Memorial Park throughout the summer, bringing the community together on warm evenings.

attractions.name: The Rock
attractions.description: A massive 570-ton glacial boulder deposited during the Ice Age that gave the borough its name. A unique geological landmark and local icon.

attractions.name: Holsten's Brookdale Confectionery
attractions.description: Famous as the location where the final scene of HBO's The Sopranos was filmed. A must-visit for fans of the iconic television series.

attractions.name: Thielke Arboretum
attractions.description: An 11-acre nature sanctuary offering guided walks, bird watching, and environmental education programs.

shoppingAreas: Rock Road — Glen Rock's main street with local shops, restaurants, banks, and services
Harding Plaza — Small shopping area with convenience stores and eateries
Nearby Ridgewood — The walkable downtown of neighboring Ridgewood offers upscale shopping and dining (1 mile)

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Monday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Glen Rock Municipal Building, 1 Harding Plaza, Glen Rock, NJ 07452

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450
